title: WBS nouns versus schedule verbs rule
overview: Provides the simple language rule for keeping a WBS deliverables-based.
detailed_summary: To ensure the WBS remains deliverables-based rather than activities-based, every element in the WBS should be represented as a noun phrase rather than a verb phrase. The rule is: WBS equals nouns, Schedule Activities equals verbs. When decomposition shifts from nouns describing a deliverable to verb phrases describing how it will be produced, the WBS has reached its lowest useful level of decomposition.
content: The simplest test for whether an item belongs in the WBS is grammatical. If the item is a noun phrase, it likely represents a deliverable and may belong in the WBS. If it is a verb phrase, it likely describes an activity and belongs in the schedule. This rule helps stakeholders maintain a clear distinction between scope definition and execution planning and signals when to transition from WBS decomposition to activity definition.
search_queries: Should WBS items be nouns or verbs?
How do I keep a WBS deliverables-based?
WBS equals nouns schedule equals verbs
common_questions: Why should WBS elements be nouns?
How do I tell if an item is an activity instead of a deliverable?
What language rule keeps WBS and schedule separate?
can_answer: How to test WBS item wording
How to distinguish deliverables from activities
When decomposition has gone far enough
examples: Frame is a WBS noun. Paint Frame is a schedule verb phrase.
best_practices: Review WBS labels and rewrite verb phrases as noun phrases where possible.
Use the nouns-versus-verbs rule during workshops to prevent activity drift.
warnings: Verb-based WBS elements indicate the team may be building a schedule instead of a WBS.
limitations: A noun phrase alone does not guarantee the item is in scope or sufficiently clear.
troubleshooting: If a WBS branch contains phrases like design, build, review, or install as actions, move them to activity planning and replace them with deliverable names.
decision_rules: If the element is a noun phrase representing an output, place it in the WBS.
If the element is a verb phrase representing an action, place it in the schedule.
